sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Excellent! Clicking on the green block at row 15, col 33 removed it! Green blocks went from 7 to 6. So the goal is to click on all green blocks to collect them. Let me continue clicking on the remaining 6 green blocks.
The remaining green blocks are:
- 3 at top: rows 1-5, cols 13-17, 19-23, 25-29
- 3 in middle: rows 13-17, cols 37-41, 43-47, 49-53
Let me click on all of them. I can batch multiple MOUSE actions.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model updated:
- MOUSE on green blocks removes them (collection mechanic confirmed)
- 6 green blocks remain after clicking one
- Goal: Click all green blocks to clear the level
- No need to move agent - just click each green block
Plan: Batch-click all remaining 6 green blocks to complete the level.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Get remaining green block positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
green_blocks = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Remaining green blocks: {len(green_blocks)}")

# Compute center of each block for clicking
click_positions = []
for n in green_blocks:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    center_row = (min(rows) + max(rows)) // 2
    center_col = (min(cols) + max(cols)) // 2
    click_positions.append((center_row, center_col))
    print(f"  Click at ({center_row}, {center_col})")

# Batch click all remaining green blocks
actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in click_positions]
result = action(actions)
print(f"\nResult: {result}")

# Verify
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
nodes2 = seg2['nodes']
green_blocks2 = [n for n in nodes2 if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Green blocks remaining: {len(green_blocks2)}")
print(f"Level completed: {result.get('level_completed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
